articles.title: Name and territory of the Union
articles.summary: India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States.
articles.example: Defines the geographical and political boundary of the country.

articles.title: Formation of new States
articles.summary: Parliament can form new states or change the boundaries of existing ones.
articles.example: The creation of Telangana from Andhra Pradesh was done using this power.

articles.title: Citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution
articles.summary: Explains who became a citizen of India when the Constitution was first adopted.
articles.example: People born in India or those with parents born in India were granted citizenship.

articles.title: Parliament to regulate the right of citizenship by law
articles.summary: Gives Parliament the power to make laws regarding citizenship and its acquisition or termination.
articles.example: The Citizenship Act of 1955 was passed using this constitutional authority.

articles.title: Equality before law
articles.summary: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law.
articles.example: A celebrity and a common man are both treated equally by the police and courts.

articles.title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech
articles.summary: Guarantees freedom of speech, assembly, association, and movement.
articles.example: A journalist can write an article criticizing government policy without being arrested for just expressing an opinion.

articles.title: Protection of life and personal liberty
articles.summary: No person shall be deprived of their life or personal liberty except according to legal procedure.
articles.example: This includes the right to privacy and the right to a clean environment as declared by courts.

articles.title: Equal justice and free legal aid
articles.summary: The State shall provide free legal aid to ensure justice for the poor.
articles.example: If you cannot afford a lawyer, you can apply for a free legal aid counsel from the DLSA.

articles.title: Uniform civil code for the citizens
articles.summary: The State shall endeavor to secure for the citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India.
articles.example: A common set of laws for marriage, divorce, and inheritance regardless of religion.
